articles.title: Opinion: Congress can rein in out-of-control prediction markets
articles.summary: Someone thinks Congress should step in to regulate prediction markets that have gotten too wild for comfort. If lawmakers actually act, it could kill the fun stuff like election betting and force platforms to stick to boring economic indicators that nobody wants to trade.

articles.title: Washington sues Kalshi as states ramp up legal pressure against prediction markets
articles.summary: Washington state filed a lawsuit against prediction market Kalshi, joining a growing wave of state-level legal challenges targeting the industry. The coordinated state pushback threatens to fragment prediction markets into a patchwork of local regulations, potentially limiting where Americans can legally bet on everything from elections to Fed rate cuts.

articles.title: The failures of prediction markets are predictable
articles.summary: Prediction markets consistently stumble over the same obstacles: thin liquidity, manipulation attempts, regulatory uncertainty, and the eternal struggle between accuracy and popularity. These recurring failures reveal systematic weaknesses that smart bettors can exploit and market operators must finally address if they want to scale beyond niche curiosities.

articles.title: 'Informed' traders on Polymarket netted $143 million in 'anomalous' profit since 2024, researchers find
articles.summary: A new study found that sophisticated traders on Polymarket made $143 million in unusual profits throughout 2024, suggesting they had access to better information or trading strategies than typical users. This confirms what many suspected about prediction markets: the house edge isn't just the platform fee, it's also getting picked off by traders who know something you don't.

articles.title: Kalshi Approved for Margin Trading as It Lures Wall Street Pros
articles.summary: Kalshi just got regulatory approval to offer margin trading, letting users borrow money to amplify their positions on prediction markets. The move signals Kalshi's serious push to attract institutional traders who demand sophisticated financial tools, potentially bringing Wall Street-level liquidity and volatility to political and economic betting.

articles.title: Tennessee vs. Iowa State Predictions: Kalshi Odds & Trade Analysis
articles.summary: Kalshi traders are weighing odds on the Tennessee vs. Iowa State matchup, with market analysis revealing where smart money is flowing. The game presents a pure test of whether college basketball prediction markets can outperform traditional sportsbooks when information asymmetries create pricing gaps.

articles.title: Dan Patrick adds data centers, prediction markets and THC to Senate priorities
articles.summary: Texas Lieutenant Governor Dan Patrick announced his legislative priorities for the upcoming session, which surprisingly include data centers, prediction markets, and THC alongside his usual conservative agenda. This signals that prediction markets could gain legitimate regulatory footing in one of America's largest states, potentially opening the floodgates for broader adoption across red states that typically follow Texas's lead.

articles.title: California bans officials from using inside knowledge to bet on prediction markets
articles.summary: California just banned government officials from betting on prediction markets using information they gain through their jobs. The move signals that regulators are taking prediction markets seriously enough to worry about insider trading, which could legitimize the space but also invite more scrutiny.

articles.title: Insider trading concerns around the Iran war are on the rise. Can anyone police the bets?
articles.summary: Traders are increasingly betting on oil prices and defense stocks ahead of major geopolitical events, raising red flags about potential insider information from government and military sources. Prediction markets face the same vulnerability since oil futures and conflict odds move on the same leaked intel that corrupts traditional securities markets.

articles.title: NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange invests $600 million in Polymarket
articles.summary: Intercontinental Exchange, which owns the New York Stock Exchange, just dropped $600M into Polymarket, the crypto-based prediction market that's been dominating election betting. This is mainstream Wall Street saying prediction markets are real financial infrastructure, not just gambling curiosities,expect more institutional money and regulatory clarity to follow.

articles.title: How Prediction Markets Scaled to USD 21B in Monthly Volume in 2026 | TRM Blog
articles.summary: Prediction markets hit $21B in monthly trading volume by 2026, marking explosive growth from today's relatively niche market. This scale puts prediction markets in the same league as major asset classes and signals they're finally breaking into mainstream finance where real money follows real insights.

articles.title: Media’s New Ethical Dilemma: Polymarket and Kalshi
articles.summary: News outlets are grappling with whether to cover prediction market odds as legitimate polling data or dismiss them as gambling noise. The decision shapes public perception of these platforms and could determine whether prediction markets gain mainstream credibility or remain niche betting venues.
